questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. WKFSKSWS. WFKLS / WFKLS / WFKLS / WFKLS

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term Kidney : Nephrology :: Clouds : ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Nephology. Study of kidney = Nephrology Study of clouds = Nephology

questions.question: Three statements are given, followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III . Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: No sugar is coffee. All coffee are biscuits. Some biscuits are Tea. Conclusions: I. Some Tea are coffee. II. Some biscuits are coffee. III. No Tea is sugar.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only conclusion II follows. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/rean%202.png) Only conclusion II follows.

questions.question: In the given question, four sets of names and address are given out of which two are relatively same. Find the two combinations and mark the correct answer accordingly? • XYZ Corp- 3rd floor, MG Road, Bangalore- 560001 • XYZ Corp- 3rd floor, MG Rd, Bengaluru- 560001 • XYZ Corp- 3rd floor, MG Road, Bangalore- 560001 • XYZ Corp- 3rd floor, MG Rd, Bangalore- 560001
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1 and 3. Option 1 is correct. As Set 1 and Set 3 of the address are relatively the same.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 246, 262, 287, 323, 372, ?
If the letters of the word CZECHOSLOVAKIA are arranged in alphabetical order, the position of how many letters will remain unchanged?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. One. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_13-02-2025_D14.png)

questions.question: Three of the four words are alike in some manner and hence form a group. Identify the word which does not belong to the group?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Sailfish. The first three are sweet edible snacks/desserts, while Sailfish is not a sweet or snack, but an animal (Fish).

questions.question: In a certain coded language PODIUM is coded as 72 and TOXIC is coded as 66. What will be the code for CALORIE in the same coded language ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 56. PODIUM → No. of letters = 6 16 + 15 + 4 + 9 + 21 + 13 = 78 - 6 = 72 CALORIE → No. of letters = 7 3 + 1 + 12 + 15 + 18 + 9 + 5 = 63 - 7 = 56

questions.question: In a certain coded language TAPE is coded as 2024820 and NOBEL is coded as 143062060. What will be the code for POCKET in the same coded language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 163094425120. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/rean%204.png) ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/rean%204.1.png)

questions.question: In the given question, a statement is given followed by two conclusions. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which of the following conclusions logically follows. Statement: Attendance in the library has dropped sharply. Conclusion: 1. Students are shifting to online resources 2. The library has reduced its operating hours.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Neither 1 nor 2 follows. Conclusion 1 is speculative Conclusion 2 is reason only. Hence, Neither 1 nor 2 follows.

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (13, 20, 49) (22, 27, 25) (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed)?
In a certain code language, ‘TEASER’ is coded as BFUSFT. How will ‘FLIGHT ’ be coded in the same language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. JMGUIH. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_13-02-2025_D12.png)

questions.question: Six friends Raghu, Avinash, Rahul, Tilak, Bipin and Suraj are sitting around a straight line facing North. Suraj sits fourth to the right of Rahul. Rahul is not sitting at the extreme end of the line. Bipin is the immediate neighbour of Suraj and Tilak. Avinash sits third to the left of Tilak. Who is sitting second to the right of Raghu ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Bipin. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/rean%206.png)

questions.question: Abhishek, Priyanka and Parul are the three children of Nitesh. Pratap is the son of Priyanka. Pratap’s maternal grandmother is Puja. Puja has one granddaughter Manisha. Suraj is the son-in-law of Parul and he has a son named Sanjeev. Lalit is the husband of Parul. How is Lalit related to Sanjeev ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Maternal Grandfather. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_13-02-2025_D13.png)

questions.question: What day of the week was 5th May 2016?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e.Thursday. 2001-2015:- Total years = 15 -1= 14 + 4 (leap years) = 18 \(\frac{18}{7}\)→ 4 extra days 2016:- Jan: 3 Feb:- 1 Mar:- 3 Apr:- 2 May:- 5 Total extra = (3 + 1 + 3 + 2 + 5) + 4 = 14 + 4 = 18 \(\frac{18}{7}\)→ 4 = Thursday Remember the code for the extra days: DAY Code Sunday 0/7 Monday 1 Tuesday 2 Wednesday 3 Thursday 4 Friday 5 Saturday 6 5th may 2016 → Thursday.

questions.question: Which of the following options will replace the * symbol to make the given equation correct ? 132*11*5*23*51*32
Find the next term in the given series: 7T, 11P, 13N, 17J, 19H, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 23D. Numbers are increasing in a prime number series starting from 7. And the sum of the number and positional value of the alphabet is 27 in each case. The next prime number is 23 And the alphabet = 27-23 = 4 = D Hence, the next term in the series will be 23D.

questions.question: 11 is related to 1328 following a certain logic. Following the same logic 9 is related to 726. What is 17 related to following the same logic? (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13, such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 4910. 11 : (11³ - 3) = 1328 9 : (9³ - 3) = 726 Similarly, 17 : (17³ - 3) = 4913 - 3 = 4910

questions.question: Some equation is provided below with symbols and mathematical relations. You have to check which of the following options is correct. If: A ∆ B = (A+B)³&ⁿᵇˢᵖ;− 3AB Find: 7∆4
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 1247. 7 ∆ 4 → (7 + 4)³&ⁿᵇˢᵖ;- 3 (7)(4) ⇒1247

questions.question: The following Venn diagram shows the number of students taking exams of three different subjects, Physics, Chemistry and Biology. Study the Venn diagram carefully and answer the question that follows: ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/rean8.png.jpg) The number of students taking exams of Physics or Chemistry but not Biology is?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 58. 23 + 35 = 58 students

questions.question: Some social issues and their solution is provided to you as a statement. You have to mark the logical deduction for the given statement. Statement: Reading fiction enhances empathy by allowing us to experience diverse perspectives.
options: Fiction books are best sellers in most bookstores.
People who read fiction are more understanding of others emotions.
Non fiction books provide factual information about history.
Fiction writing is a creative process requiring imagination.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. People who read fiction are more understanding of others emotions. Option 2 follows as the reading function helps people understand other feelings as given in the statement.

questions.question: Select the most logical option that arranges the given words in a logical sequence. Start engine, Fasten seat belts, Adjust mirror, Check fuel level, Drive?
options: Start engine, Adjust mirror, Fasten seat belts, Check fuel level, Drive.
Start engine, Check fuel level, Fasten seat belts, Drive, Adjust mirror.
Check fuel level, Adjust mirror, Fasten seat belts, Start engine, Drive.
Start engine, Adjust mirror, Check fuel level, Fasten seat belts, Drive.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Check fuel level, Adjust mirror, Fasten seat belts, Start engine, Drive. Option 3 arranges the logical order of the words.

questions.question: The ratio of molars to pre-molars in adult humans is ____.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 3 : 2. The permanent dentition consists of 32 teeth in total with the dental formula 2123/2123, indicating two incisor, one canine, two premolar and three molar 3 : 2 in each quadrant.

questions.question: With reference to the issue of marital rape in India, consider the following statements: • Under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, non-consensual sexual acts within marriage are excluded from the definition of rape if the wife is above 16 years of age. • The Justice Verma Committee has recommended removing the marital rape exception. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2 only. • Under Section 63 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, marital rape continues to be excluded as a criminal offence. This implies that non-consensual sexual intercourse within marriage is not considered rape if the wife is above 18 years of age. • It has been argued that this exception violates Article 21 of the Constitution, which guarantees the right to life, dignity, privacy, and bodily autonomy. • According to the National Family Health Survey-5 (NFHS-5), nearly 83% of sexual violence against women is perpetrated by husbands rather than strangers. • The Justice Verma Committee recommended the removal of the marital rape exception. Similarly, international conventions such as CEDAW, to which India is a signatory, advocate for the recognition of marital rape as an offence. • However, previous Law Commission reports have expressed the view that criminalising marital rape may not be consistent with India’s prevailing social conditions.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: • Makhana is an aquatic crop and is mainly grown in tropical and subtropical regions. • In 2022, ‘Mithila Makhana’ was conferred a Geographical Indication (GI) tag. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both 1 and 2. Statements 1 and 2 are correct : Makhana, commonly known in English as fox nut, is the dried edible seed of the prickly water lily or gorgon plant (Euryale ferox). • This plant is found in freshwater ponds throughout South and East Asia. • It is recognized for its violet and white flowers, as well as its large, round, and prickly leaves, which can often exceed a meter in diameter. • The edible part of the Makhana plant consists of small, round seeds that have an outer layer ranging from black to brown. This has led to it being referred to as the ‘Black Diamond.’ • In 2022, ‘Mithila Makhana’ was conferred a Geographical Indication (GI) tag. • Makhana (Gorgon Nut or Foxnut) is an aquatic crop and is mainly grown in tropical and subtropical regions.

questions.question: A typical adult human body contains about ______ g of magnesium.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 25. An adult body contains approximately 25g magnesium, with 50% to 60% present in the bones and most of the rest in soft tissues. Less than 1% of total magnesium is in blood serum, and these levels are kept under tight control.

questions.question: In which year did the Dalai Lama cross over into India and take asylum?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 1959. The Dalai Lama is the head of state and Spiritual Tibetan religious leader of Tibet (Tenjin Gyatso) was born on July 6, 1935 in North east Tibet. The Dalai Lama took refuge in India at the age of 24 on March 31, 1959, after launching and armed invasion of Tibet by China.

questions.question: In which year did the Indian Premier League (IPL) start?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 2008. Indian Premier League (IPL) was started in the year 2008. A total 8 team participated in this event. IPL 2008 season was won by Rajasthan Royals.

questions.question: With reference to Ellora Caves, which of the following statements is/are correct? • They were carved between 600 CE and 1000 CE under the patronage of multiple dynasties. • The site contains Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain caves, reflecting religious coexistence. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both 1 and 2. • Located in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Maharashtra, the Ellora Caves are among India’s first UNESCO World Heritage sites. • The cave complex was carved between 600 CE and 1000 CE, spanning the rule of the Chalukyas, Rashtrakutas, and Yadavas. • Religious Pluralism: The complex comprises 34 major caves, 12 Buddhist, 17 Hindu, and 5 Jain, demonstrating a unique period of interreligious harmony and artistic exchange.

questions.question: How many field umpires are there in a cricket match?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2. There are a total of three (3) umpires in a cricket match. In which there are 2 field umpires. Only the field umpire takes important decisions. The job of the umpire is to give decisions impartially and without discrimination.

questions.question: When is the National Consumer Rights Day observed in India every year?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 24 December. National Consumer Rights Day is observed on 24 December every year in India. The day aims to highlight the importance of the consumer movement as well as the need to make them aware and informed of their rights and responsibilities. On this day in the year 1986, the Consumer Protection Act 1986 came into force after receiving the Presidential Assent.

questions.question: Match the following: List I (Mahajanapada) List II (Capital) A) Magadha 1) Ujjaini B) Avanti 2) Rajagriha C) Vatsa 3) Kaushambi D) Gandhara 4) Taxila Select the correct answer using the code below:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4. Mahajanapada Capital Anga Champa Assaka Potana Avanti Ujjayini Chedi Suktimati Gandhara Taxila Kamboja Rajapura Kashi Varanasi Kosala Ayodhya Kuru Indraprastha Magadha Rajgriha Malla Kushinagar Matsya Viratanagara Panchala Ahichhatra Surasena Mathura Vajji Vaishali Vatsa Kausambi

questions.question: Under which Amendment Act, the scope of the electoral college has been increased by reducing the age limit to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 61st. 61st Amendment in India, every adult citizen above the age of 18 years gets a chance to vote. This age limit was 21 years until the 61st amendment was introduced to the Constitution in 1988.

questions.question: Barium loses………. electrons to achieve noble gas electronic configuration.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2. An ideal (noble) gas is characterized by its high stability. The stability of an element is determined by the number of electrons in its outermost shell. Elements that have eight electrons in their valence shell are considered stable because they attain a complete octet configuration. The electronic configuration of barium is: 1s², 2s², 2p⁶, 3s², 3p⁶, 4s², 3d¹⁰, 4p⁶, 5s², 4d¹⁰, 5p⁶, 6s². From this configuration, we can see that barium has two electrons in its outermost (sixth) shell. By losing these two electrons, it attains a stable noble gas configuration with eight electrons in its new outer shell.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a condition for President’s Rule in a State under Article 356?
options: Breakdown of law and order in the State
Failure of constitutional machinery in the State
The State government acting against the provisions of the Constitution
Inability to elect a Chief Minister
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Breakdown of law and order in the State. Law and order problems alone do not automatically mean constitutional breakdown. • Law and order is primarily a State subject. • Disturbances, riots, or unrest can occur even when the constitutional system is still functioning. • The Centre must show that governance cannot be carried on according to the Constitution, not merely that the situation is difficult. The Supreme Court (S.R. Bommai case) clarified that Article 356 cannot be used for normal administrative failures.

questions.question: One calorie of heat energy is equivalent to approximately .................. joules of mechanical energy.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 4.2. The ability to do work is called energy. Calorie and Joule both are units of energy. Joule is a derived unit of energy or amount of heat in the International System of Units and Calorie is a unit of energy. 1 Calorie = 4.184 Joules, which is approximately equal to 4.2 Joules.

questions.question: What is the Steam Point on the Fahrenheit scale?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 212º F. Steam point of Fahrenheit scale is 212º F Ice Point (Freezing Point): 32°F (0°C)

questions.question: Who among the following introduced the Dagh and Huliya systems in the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Alauddin Khalji. He introduced the Dagh and Huliya systems to strengthen and control his army. During his rule, the Delhi Sultanate faced many Mongol attacks and internal rebellions, so he needed a strong and disciplined military. The Dagh system meant branding the horses of soldiers with a special royal mark. This was done to make sure that soldiers did not replace good horses with weak ones and still take full salary. The Huliya system involved keeping a written record of each soldier’s physical appearance, such as height and facial features. This helped prevent fake soldiers from being shown in army records to take extra salary. These reforms reduced corruption and made the army more efficient. The other rulers mentioned in the options did not introduce these systems.

questions.question: With reference to the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), consider the following statements: • PMKVY was launched in 2015 under the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME). • PMKVY primarily targets providing skill training and certification to enhance youth employability. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2 only. PMKVY launched in 2015 under the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE). • Implementing Agency: National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC). • Objective: To provide skill training and certification to youth to enhance employability. • Beneficiaries (till June 2025): Over 1.64 crore youth trained. • Budget (FY 2024–25): ₹1,538 crore. • Latest Phase: PMKVY 4.0 (2022–2026) – focuses on industry-linked courses, digital monitoring, and local-level skill hubs.

questions.question: With reference to Aravalli Range, which of the following statements is/are correct? • The Aravalli Range passes through Gujarat, Rajasthan, Haryana, and ends in Delhi. • It is the oldest mountain range in India, acting as a natural barrier preventing the eastward expansion of the Thar Desert. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both 1 and 2. The Supreme Court, in a recent order, adopted a uniform definition of the Aravalli hills and ranges and put a hold on the granting of new mining leases in Delhi, Haryana, Rajasthan, and Gujarat. Significance of the Aravalli Range: • The Aravalli Range extends for about 692 kilometres (430 miles) in a north-eastern direction. It passes through the states of Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Haryana, and finally reaches Delhi. It is considered the oldest mountain range in India, with nearly two-thirds of its length located in Rajasthan. • The range serves as an important ecological shield by restricting the eastward spread of the Thar Desert into Haryana, Rajasthan, and western Uttar Pradesh. • The Aravallis play a vital role in groundwater recharge and are the origin of rivers such as the Sabarmati and the Luni. • The region is also mineral-rich, containing resources like sandstone, limestone, marble, granite, lead, zinc, copper, gold, and tungsten.

questions.question: According to the union budget 2026-27, how many individuals have been lifted out of multidimensional poverty as of the document date?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 25 crore. During the presentation of the Union Budget 2026-27 on February 1, 2026, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that close to 25 crore (specifically 24.82 crore) individuals have been lifted out of multidimensional poverty over the last decade. This progress was attributed to the government's sustained and reform-oriented efforts, including targeted welfare programs and significant initiatives implemented between 2013-14 and 2022-23.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI): • It was established on the recommendation of the Malimath Committee. • The CBI is a statutory body created under the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act, 1946. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Neither 1 nor 2. About the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI):- It is India’s specialized investigative agency, responsible for tackling high-profile crimes, corruption, and national security matters. Origins & Evolution:- • The CBI traces its roots to the Special Police Establishment (SPE), formed in 1941 to investigate corruption in wartime procurement. • It was officially established by an executive order of the Government of India in 1963, on the recommendation of Santhanam Committee. • It was not created by an Act of Parliament, thus not a Statutory Body.

questions.question: With reference to Fundamental Rights in the Constitution of India, consider the following statements: • Fundamental Right to Property was completely removed from the Constitution by the 44th Amendment Act, 1978. • Article 13 of the Constitution declares that laws inconsistent with Fundamental Rights shall be void. • All fundamental Rights can be suspended during a proclamation of Emergency. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2 only. • Fundamental Right to Property was not completely removed. It was removed as a Fundamental Right (Article 31) but continued as a legal right under Article 300A after the 44th Amendment. • Article 13 states that laws inconsistent with Fundamental Rights are void. • During Emergency, only some parts of Fundamental Rights (like Personal Liberty under Article 21) can be suspended in certain emergencies, but not all Fundamental Rights are automatically suspended (e.g., Article 20 and 21 have special protections).

questions.question: With reference to the Current Account, consider the following statements: • A current account deficit may indicate strong domestic demand and the presence of attractive investment opportunities within the economy. • A country can maintain or even increase its foreign exchange reserves if capital inflows are greater than the current account deficit. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both 1 and 2 Statement 1 is correct. • A Current Account Deficit (CAD) typically arises when a country imports more goods and services than it exports. • It may occur due to strong domestic demand, which leads to higher imports. • It can also reflect robust investment opportunities, as foreign investors finance domestic investment, often resulting in increased imports of capital goods. Statement 2 is correct. • Even when a country runs a current account deficit, its foreign exchange reserves can still rise if capital inflows (such as FDI, portfolio investments, external borrowings, etc.) exceed the size of the CAD. • This happens because the overall Balance of Payments (BoP) remains in surplus.

questions.question: In which of the following Indian states is the Makaravilakku Festival celebrated?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Kerala. Makara Vilakku is one of the most sacred festivals of Kerala, celebrated every year at the Sabarimala Ayyappa Temple. It is observed on the first day of the Malayalam month of Makaram, which coincides with Makara Sankranti. On this auspicious occasion, thousands of devotees gather to witness two significant events: • Makara Jyoti – the appearance of the bright celestial star (Sirius) in the eastern sky. • Makara Vilakku – the sacred light that is seen three times from the forests of Ponnambalamedu. These two phenomena are regarded as deeply spiritual experiences, symbolizing the divine presence, blessings, and grace of Lord Ayyappa.

questions.question: With reference to the Arya Samaj, consider the following statements: • Arya Samaj was founded by Swami Dayanand Saraswati in 1875 as a Hindu reformist movement. • It promoted inter-caste and interfaith marriages and allowed conversions through a “shuddhi” (purification) ritual. Which of the statements given above is/are not correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Neither 1 nor 2. Both statements are correct. Arya Samaj was founded by Swami Dayanand Saraswati in 1875 as a Hindu reform movement aimed at social and religious reform. It encouraged inter-caste and interfaith marriages and permitted religious conversions through the “shuddhi” (purification) ceremony. Furthermore, the Arya Marriage Validation Act, 1937 granted legal recognition to such marriages, including those between individuals of different castes or religions, provided that both parties declared themselves followers of the Arya Samaj.

questions.question: With reference to Vande Mataram, which of the following statements is/are correct? • Vande Mataram was composed by Bankim Chandra Chatterjee and first appeared in the novel Kapalkundala in 1882. • It was first sung by Rabindranath Tagore at the 1896 Indian National Congress session. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2 only. • Vande Mataram was written in Sanskrit by Bankim Chandra Chatterjee and was first published in his 1882 novel Anandamath. The novel is set in the context of the Bengal Famine of 1769–73 and the Sanyasi Rebellion. The song gained nationwide recognition when Rabindranath Tagore sang it at the 1896 session of the Indian National Congress. • The composition glorifies the motherland as a symbol of strength, prosperity, and divinity, and it became a powerful expression of India’s emerging national consciousness.

questions.question: If a chord of a circle subtends an angle of 45° at the circumference of the circle, then what is the ratio of diameter of circle and the length of the chord.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. \(\sqrt2:1\). Let AB is a chord, subtends 45° angle at the circumference and centre of the circle is O. Therefore angle subtends by the chord at the centre= 2× 45= 90° ∆OAB is an isosceles right angle triangle Let radius be r, then diameter will be 2r and the length of the chord = r√2 Required ratio = 2r : r √2 = √2 : 1

questions.question: What amount will be received from the sum of Rs. 8760 for 7 years. if the rate of simple interest is 2% yearly.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs. 9986.40. SI =\( \frac{8760\times7\times2}{100}\)= Rs. 1226.40 Required amount= 8760 + 1226.40 = Rs. 9986.40

questions.question: Solve (cosec 30° - tan45°) cot60° tan30° = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. \(\frac{1}{3}\). (cosec 30° - tan45°) cot60° tan30° = ? = (2 - 1)\(\times\frac{1}{\sqrt3}\times\frac{1}{\sqrt3}\) =\(\frac{1}{3}\)

questions.question: In how many ways can a group of 6 people sit around a circular table if two particular persons always sit together?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 48. Let the two persons who must sit together be A and B. Treat (AB) as one block. Now total objects around the circle: (AB) block 4 other persons Total = 5 objects For circular arrangement: (n-1)! = (5-1)! = 4! = 24 arrangement possible A and B can sit as: AB, BA So, ways = 2 Total ways = 24 ×2 = 48

questions.question: The length and breadth of a rectangle are in the ratio of 2:3 and its area is equal to the area of a rhombus of side 20 cm. If one of the diagonals of rhombus is 24 cm. Find the perimeter of the rectangle.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 80. Since the diagonals of rhombus bisect each other at right angle, Find another diagonal of rhombus using triplets: 12, 16, 20 So other diagonal = 16×2= 32 cm Area of rhombus= ½ × 32 × 24 = 384 cm² Let the sides of rectangle = 2a and 3a So, 2a× 3a= 384 6a²= 384 a = 8 The sides are = 16 and 24 Perimeter= 2 × (16 + 24)= 80 cm

questions.question: In a 200 m race, A can give 20 m start to B, and A can give 40 m start to C. In a race of 180 m, how much start can B give to C?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 20. Given: A : B = 200 : 180 = 10 : 9 A : C = 200 : 160 = 10 : 8 ∴ B : C = 9 : 8 In a 180 m race, when B runs 180 m, C runs 8/9 × 180 = 160m Therefore, start given by B to C = 180 − 160 = 20 m

questions.question: Find the LCM of 0.12, 0.18 and 0.45.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 1.8. Multiply all numbers by 100 New numbers are 12, 18, 45 LCM of (12, 18, 45) = 180 So the required LCM = 180/100 = 1.8 ans

questions.question: Two pipes A and B, working together, can fill an empty tank in 24 minutes. The ratio of their capacities is 2 : 3. Find how much more time pipe A will take than pipe B to fill the tank individually.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 20. Ratio of capacity= 2:3 Capacity of tank = 5 × 24 = 120 Time taken by A = 120 /2= 60 min Time taken by B = 120/3= 40 Difference = 60-40= 20 minutes

questions.question: Seema flies a kite on a 16 m string at an inclination of 60°. What is the height of the kite above the ground?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. \(8\sqrt3\). Let the height of the kite is h. sin60°=\(\frac{h}{16}\) \(\frac{\sqrt3\ }{2}=\frac{h}{16}\) h= \(8\sqrt3\)

questions.question: In an election contested by three candidates A, B and C, 10% of the total votes were invalid. Of the valid votes, A got 40%, B got 25%, C got the remaining votes. After the election, it was found that 20% of the votes polled in favour of A were invalidly counted, and after correcting this mistake, the number of votes received by C remained the same, and C defeated A by 1,728 votes. Find the total number of votes polled in the election.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 64000. Let total number of votes = 1000 So, total valid votes = 900 A got = 900 × 40% = 360 C got = 900 × 35 % = 315 But it is given that, 20% of votes polled in favour of A were invalidly counted. So total valid votes polled in favour of A = 360 × 80% = 288 Difference of A & C = 315- 288= 27 unit 27 unit =1728 1 unit = 64 1000 unit = 64000

questions.question: The mean of a data set is 30 and the median is 28. Using the empirical relation between mean, median and mode, find the mode of the data.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 24. Empirical relation = 3 Median = Mode + 2 Mean 3× 28 = Mode + 2× 30 Mode = 84- 60 = 24

questions.question: For a distribution, the mean is 50, the standard deviation is 10, and the mode is 44. Find Karl Pearson’s coefficient of skewness.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 0.6. Karl Pearson’s coefficient of skewness =\( \frac{Mean-\ Mode}{Standard\ deviation}=\frac{50-44}{10}\) = 0.6

questions.question: Divide Rs. 4720 among A, B and C such that A, B and C receive money in the ratio ⅕:⅙:⅛. Find the difference of amount received by A and C.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 720. A: B C = ⅕ : ⅙ : ⅛ = 120/5 : 120/6 : 120/8 = 24 : 20 : 15 Difference between A and C = 4720 × \(\frac 9{59}\) = 720

questions.question: Two circles of equal radius intersect such that each circle passes through the centre of the other. If the radius of each circle is 12 cm, find the length of the common chord of the two circles.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. \(12\sqrt3\). Note- if two circles of equal radius r, intersect such that each circle passes through the centre of the other. then the length of the common chord of the two circles= r\(\sqrt3\) Therefore required answer = \(12\sqrt3\) cm

questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: Every integer is a rational number.
Zero is a whole number, an integer, and a rational number.
The product of a non-zero rational number and an irrational number is always irrational.
The product of two irrational numbers is always irrational.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. The product of two irrational numbers is always irrational. Statement 4 is incorrect. The product of two irrational numbers is either rational or irrational. Ex: √2 × √2 = 2 which is rational

questions.question: A vessel contains 120 litres of milk. From the vessel, 12 litres of milk is taken out and replaced with water. Again 15 litres of milk is taken out and replaced with water and at last 20 litres of milk is taken out and replaced with water. Find the quantity of milk left in the vessel after the third replacement.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 78.75 liter. Milk left after third replacement= = \(120\ \times\frac{108}{120}\ \times\frac{105}{120}\ \times\frac{100}{120}\) = 78.75 liter

questions.question: A grocer has a sale of Rs. 6435, Rs. 6925, Rs. 6800, Rs. 7100 and Rs. 7250 in 5 consecutive days. How much sale must he have in the sixth day so that he gets an average sale of Rs. 6900.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs 6890. Total sale of 5 days : 6435 + 6925 + 6800 + 7100 + 7250 = 34510 Total sales of 6 days : 6 × 6900 = 41400 Sale of 6th day = 41400 - 34510 = Rs 6890

questions.question: Aman bought an article for 1560 and sold it at a loss of 25%. With this amount, he bought another article and sold it at a gain of 40%. His overall percentage profit is?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 5%. 25% loss = \(- \frac{1}{4\ }\) , 40% profit = +\(\frac{2}{5}\) C.P. → S.P. 4 3 5 7 20 21 Profit = S.P.- C.P. = 21 - 20 = 1 Profit%= \(\frac{1}{20}\times100\)=5%

questions.question: If x + 3y + 2 = 0 then value of x³&ⁿᵇˢᵖ;+ 27y³&ⁿᵇˢᵖ;+ 8 - 18xy is
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 0. Given, x + 3y + 2 = 0 x + 3y = -2 Cube both sides \(\ x^3+27y^3+9xy\left(x+3y\right)=\ -8\) \(\ x^3+27y^3-18xy+8 =0\)

questions.question: A circle is inscribed in ∆ABC touching AB at P, BC at Q and AC at R. If AR= 10 cm, RC= 12 cm and AB = 24 cm, then the perimeter of ∆ABC is
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 72. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_13-02-2025_D1.png) With the help of tangent property both tangent of a circle drawn from a same external point are equal. AP = AR, BP = BQ, CR = CQ Perimeter of ∆ABC AB + BC + AC Or (AP + PB) (BQ + QC) (AR + RC) 24 + 14 + 12 + 10 + 12 = 72

questions.question: A train crosses a pole in 24 sec, and a bridge of length 360 m in 36 sec. Then the speed of the train is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 30 m/s. Let length of train = T Let speed of train = S \(\frac{T}{S\ }=24\) sec, T= 24 S \(\frac{T+360}{S}\)=36 sec 24S+ 360 = 36S S= \(\frac{360}{12}\) m/sec = 30 m/sec SHORT – The train takes extra (36-24) =12 Minutes to cover extra distance of 360 m. So, Speed of train = \(\frac{360}{12}\)=30 m/s

questions.question: At what rate per annum will ₹33,000 yield a compound interest of ₹31,680 in 2 years, interest being compounded yearly?
Five cubes, each of edge 3 cm are joined end to end. What is the total surface area of the resulting cuboid, in cm²?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 198. L = 3 × 5 = 15 l = 15, b = 3 h = 3 Total surface are of cuboid = 2(lb + bh + hl) = 2(45 + 9 + 45) 198 cm²

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells his goods by allowing 20% discount and still earns 30% profit. If he sells his goods by allowing 15% discount on the marked price, then what will be his profit percentage?(correct to 2 decimal point)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 38.13%. Let MP = 100 Discount %= 20% Then SP = 80 If Discount % = 15% Then SP = 85 \(\therefore\) SP = SP \(\because \) 80 \(\rightarrow\)130 \(\therefore\) 85 \(\rightarrow\)\(\frac{130}{80}\times85\) = 138.125 =138.125 - 100 = 38.125%

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. The manager is discussing. Error Concept: Incorrect Tense Usage [from the chapter: Tense] 🔍 Grammatical Concept: When an action began in the past and continues into the present, and is followed by "since" or "for", it requires the present perfect continuous tense, not the present continuous. 🔧 How to correct it: Replace “is discussing” with “has been discussing”. ✔ Correct Sentence: The manager has been discussing the new policy with staff since the early morning hours.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The critics continued to __________ the movie, calling it unrealistic and poorly acted.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Deride. Explanation: The sentence clearly shows a harsh, mocking tone toward the movie, describing it as unrealistic and poorly acted. The word required should reflect ridicule or scorn. Deride fits perfectly, as it means to mock or treat with contempt. Let’s see the meaning of all the key words: • Deride – मज़ाक उड़ाना / उपहास करना – To mock or express scorn and contempt for someone or something. Example: “His classmates derided him for his unusual accent.” • Beguile – बहकाना / मोहित करना – To charm or deceive someone in a clever or attractive way. Example: “She was beguiled by his smooth words.” • Desecrate – अपवित्र करना – To disrespect or violate something sacred. Example: “The vandals desecrated the holy site.” • Indict – अभियोग लगाना – To formally accuse someone of a serious crime. Example: “The jury decided to indict the official for corruption.” Since the critics are mocking the movie, not charming it, violating it as sacred, or accusing it of a crime, Option [4] Deride is the most logical and contextually accurate choice.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the following sentence. The chameleon survived in the wild by using its modesty and staying anxious of predators at all times.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Camouflage, Wary. Explanation: The sentence describes how the chameleon survives in the wild by using its natural ability to hide and by being cautious of predators. The original words “modesty” and “anxious” are not accurate for describing these survival traits. Let’s understand all the key words used in the question and options: • Camouflage – छिपाने की कला / छिपाव – The ability of an animal to blend into its environment to avoid detection by predators. Example: “The chameleon uses camouflage to match the color of the leaves.” • Wary – सतर्क / सावधान – Being watchful and cautious, especially to avoid danger or trouble. Example: “The deer is wary of hunters nearby.” • Modesty – विनम्रता / शालीनता – Humility or a reserved nature; does not relate to hiding or survival skills. Example: “Her modesty made her well-liked.” • Anxious – चिंतित / तनावग्रस्त – Feeling worried or uneasy, usually about something uncertain or threatening; it describes an emotional state rather than a cautious survival behavior. Example: “She was anxious before the job interview.” • Disguise – भेष / नकाब – A method of concealing one’s identity or appearance, similar to camouflage but often implies deliberate deception. Example: “The spy wore a disguise to avoid detection.” • Fear – डर / भय – An emotional response to perceived danger or threat. Example: “The child was overcome with fear during the thunderstorm.” • Serenity – शांति / सुकून – Calmness and peacefulness; opposite of anxious or worried. Example: “She found serenity by meditating.” • Weary – थका हुआ / थकावट – Physically or mentally tired; different from wary, which means cautious. Example: “After the long hike, he felt weary.” ✅So, Option [3] Camouflage, Wary best fits the meaning and tone of the sentence.

questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence.
options: She is not only intelligent but also works very sincerely.
He not only completed the report but also was presenting it.
They not only arrived late but also missed the briefing.
The officer not only strict but also disciplined.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. They not only arrived late but also missed the briefing. CONCEPT – Parallelism with “Not only … but also” ✅ • The correlative conjunction “not only … but also” is used to join two similar grammatical structures. • Whatever grammatical form appears after not only, the same form must appear after but also. • A noun must be balanced with a noun, a verb with a verb, a phrase with a phrase, and a clause with a clause. • Lack of parallel structure makes the sentence grammatically incorrect. ✔ Hence, only the sentence that correctly follows parallel structure on both sides of “not only … but also” without any grammatical violation is Option (3). Correct Sentence – “They not only arrived late but also missed the briefing.” ✅ WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG ❌ Option (1) is incorrect because “intelligent” (adjective) is paired with “works” (verb), which breaks parallelism. ❌ Option (2) is incorrect because “completed” (simple past) is paired with “was presenting” (past continuous). Both sides must have the same tense and form. ❌ Option (4) is incorrect because the verb “is” is missing before disciplined, and the structure is incomplete. Exam Tip 📝 - Whenever you see not only … but also, immediately compare the structure after both parts. If they are not grammatically identical, eliminate that option.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Hygienic. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Echelon - पायदान - a level or rank in a system or hierarchy Example - The company has a clear echelon system for promotions. Recruitment - भर्ती - the process of finding and hiring new employees Example - The recruitment process for the new company took several weeks. Hygienic - स्वच्छ - clean and free from dirt, germs, or other contaminants Example - The restaurant maintained hygienic conditions to ensure customer safety. Rebellious - विद्रोही - showing a desire to resist or fight against authority Example - The rebellious teenager refused to follow the rules.

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no correction is needed, select ‘No correction required’. The rich have always enjoyed privileges that others do not have.
options: The rich man have always enjoyed
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. No correction required. ❌ Error Concept: Misunderstanding of subject-verb agreement and plural usage with collective nouns like "the rich." ✅ Grammatical Concept: "The rich" is a collective noun that refers to a group of people who are wealthy. In English, collective nouns like "the rich," "the poor," and "the elderly" are treated as plural nouns. • “The rich” = plural subject [refers to all wealthy people] • Therefore, it correctly takes the plural verb “have” • The rest of the sentence follows standard grammar: “enjoyed privileges that others do not have.” In the sentence: “The rich have always enjoyed privileges that others do not have.” The subject “The rich” is correctly paired with the plural verb “have” and the rest of the sentence is grammatically correct. It can be said that option [4] No correction required is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The team’s early momentum has dissipated due to wicket of Rohit Sharma.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Fritter. Let’s know the meaning of all words: Dissipated - समाप्त होना, विखर जाना - to disappear or fade away Example -The team's early momentum dissipated after the key player's injury, leading to a significant decline in their performance. Secluded - अलग-थलग - isolated or hidden Example - The village was secluded and difficult to reach, surrounded by dense forests and winding roads that only a few locals knew well. Fritter - व्यर्थ में खर्च करना - to waste or squander Example - She frittered away her entire month's salary on unnecessary shopping sprees and expensive dinners, leaving her with barely enough for the rest of the month. Ramify - शाखाएँ फैलाना - to branch out or have consequences Example - The new policy will ramify across various departments, affecting workflow, budget allocations, and staffing requirements, necessitating comprehensive training for all employees. Disqualify - अयोग्य ठहराना - to declare someone or something as not eligible Example - The athlete was disqualified from the competition due to a doping violation, a decision that was upheld after a lengthy appeal process.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Putrefy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. decontaminate. Let’ see the meaning of all words: Putrefy - सड़ना - to rot or decay Example - The fish putrefied in the warm water, emitting a foul smell that was unbearable. Deteriorate - खराब होना - to become worse Example - The building's condition deteriorated over time due to lack of maintenance. Astute - चतुर - clever or perceptive Example - She was an astute businesswoman, making smart investments and deals. Vigilant - चौकस - watchful or alert Example - The security team was vigilant, monitoring the premises 24/7. Decontaminate – संदूषण मुक्त करना - to remove contamination or impurities Example - The team worked to decontaminate the area after the chemical spill

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. I wondered what the ___________ in her coat pocket was.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Bulge. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Bulge – उभार - A protrusion or swelling; a visible or noticeable lump or curve Example - She noticed a strange bulge in her bag and wondered what it could be. Banal - नीरस - Lacking originality or interest - Lacking originality or imagination; dull Example - The comedian's jokes were so banal that the audience barely laughed. Lavish - असाधारण - Extravagant or luxurious in expenditure or decoration Example: The lavish decorations at the wedding reception were impressive. Hitch - अड़चन - A problem or difficulty that needs to be overcome Example: The hitch in the plan was that they didn't have enough funding.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Give someone the benefit of the doubt
options: To assume someone’s intentions or actions are good or correct, even when there is uncertainty
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. To assume someone’s intentions or actions are good or correct, even when there is uncertainty. Explanation: The idiom “give someone the benefit of the doubt” means to choose to trust or believe in someone’s good intentions when you are not completely sure about the facts. It reflects a fair and generous attitude instead of immediate suspicion. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: The phrase comes from legal and moral reasoning, where a person is considered innocent until proven guilty. Over time, it entered everyday usage to express giving someone a fair chance. Example: Although his explanation sounded strange, I decided to give him the benefit of the doubt. Hindi: “give someone the benefit of the doubt” = संदेह होने पर भी किसी पर विश्वास करना / किसी को संदेह का लाभ देना

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: Now he is worried about it.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. He should complete the assignment. Error Concept: Incorrect Use of Modal Verb + Tense Consistency [from the chapter Modal Auxiliaries] Grammatical Concept: • The modal phrase “should complete” indicates present or future obligation or advice. • However, the sentence refers to a past event [indicated by “yesterday” and “didn’t”]. • When referring to a past obligation that was not fulfilled, the modal verb “should” is combined with “have” + past participle [perfect infinitive]. • Therefore, “should complete” is incorrect for past reference; it should be “should have completed”. How to correct it: Replace “should complete” with “should have completed” to correctly express an unfulfilled past obligation. Correct Sentence: “He should have completed the assignment yesterday, but he didn’t. Now he is worried about it.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Chary
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Gingerly. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Chary - सावधान, सतर्क - cautious or wary, often due to potential risks or uncertainties Example: She was chary of investing in new ventures without thorough research. Gingerly -सावधानी से और धीरे-धीरे काम करना - doing something in a cautious and careful manner. Example: She handled the fragile item gingerly to avoid breaking it. Rash - लापरवाह - acting or done without careful thought or consideration, often leading to undesirable outcomes Example: His rash decision led to unforeseen consequences. Audacious - निडर - showing a willingness to take bold risks or challenge conventional norms, often with confidence and courage Example: The audacious plan required courage and determination. Trial - परीक्षण - a test or examination of something, such as a product, idea, or person's abilities, to assess its quality, effectiveness, or suitability Example: The new product is currently in trial phase.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The motivational speaker's words were meant to exalt the audience's spirits and inspire them to pursue their dreams.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Denigrate. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Exalt - प्रशंसा करना, उत्कर्ष - to praise or glorify someone or something, or to elevate or raise something to a higher level Example: The motivational speaker's words were meant to exalt the audience's spirits and inspire them to pursue their dreams. Ennoble - उदात्त करना - to give someone or something a noble quality Example: The philanthropic work ennobled her reputation in the community. Denigrate - निंदा करना - to criticize or belittle someone or something Example: The politician's opponents tried to denigrate her achievements during the campaign. Extol - प्रशंसा करना - to praise or glorify someone or something Example: The critic extolled the virtues of the new film, calling it a masterpiece. Oust - हटाना - to remove someone from a position or place Example: The board voted to oust the CEO due to poor performance.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The council approved a __________ overhaul of the traffic system because of the increasing accidents occurring in close __________ to schools.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. radical, proximity. Explanation: The sentence discusses a serious response [an overhaul] to increasing accidents near schools. This suggests a major change was needed because of how close the incidents were to sensitive areas [schools]. So, the correct words must express: • a major change [→ radical] • closeness [→ proximity] Let’s look at the meanings of all key words used in the options: [1] radical, proximity ✅ • Radical – मौलिक / अत्यधिक – Extreme or major in nature; a fundamental change. Example: “They proposed a radical reform of the tax system.” • Proximity – निकटता / पास होना – Nearness in space, time, or relationship. Example: “The park’s proximity to the school makes it popular with students.” This pair perfectly matches the context — a serious change due to closeness to schools. [2] gradual, separation ❌ • Gradual – धीरे-धीरे होने वाला – Something that happens slowly over time. Example: “The gradual decline in sales was hard to notice at first.” • Separation – अलगाव / दूरी – The state of being apart or far from something. Example: “The separation between the buildings reduced noise transfer.” These words imply slow change and distance, which contradict the urgency and closeness mentioned in the sentence. [3] minor, remoteness ❌ • Minor – मामूली / छोटा – Small or insignificant. Example: “It was just a minor injury.” • Remoteness – दूरदराज़ / दूरी – The quality of being far away. Example: “The remoteness of the village makes it hard to access.” A minor change would not match the overhaul, and remoteness is the opposite of proximity. [4] partial, distance ❌ • Partial – आंशिक / अधूरा– Incomplete or not whole. Example: “The plan received only partial approval.” • Distance – दूरी – The amount of space between two things. Example: “He kept a safe distance from the fire.” A partial overhaul contradicts the idea of a comprehensive fix. And distance doesn’t align with “close to schools.” So, the only option that fits both meaning and tone of the sentence is: [1] radical, proximity

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A person who gives help and sympathy to people who need it
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Samaritan. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Samaritan - परोपकारी व्यक्ति - a person who helps others in need, often in a selfless manner. Example: The Good Samaritan stopped to help the stranded driver change her tire. Waif - अनाथ या असहाय व्यक्ति - a person, especially a child, who is homeless or without proper care. Example: The charity worked to provide shelter and food for the waifs living on the streets. Pacifism - शांतिवाद - the belief that violence should be avoided and that disputes should be settled peacefully. Example: The activist's commitment to pacifism led her to participate in non-violent protests. Coronation - राज्याभिषेक - the ceremony of crowning a king or queen. Example: The coronation of the new king was attended by dignitaries from around the world.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Second wind
options: To make a desperate attempt to salvage a bad situation
Another burst of energy when you had been too tired to finish
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Another burst of energy when you had been too tired to finish. Explanation: The idiom “second wind” refers to a sudden return of strength or energy after a period of fatigue, especially when someone feels too tired to continue but then unexpectedly finds new energy. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: The expression comes from athletics and long-distance running. Runners often feel exhausted at first, but after continuing for some time, they experience a fresh surge of energy, known as a second wind. Example: I was ready to quit, but I got a second wind and completed the task. Hindi: “second wind” = थकान के बाद नई ऊर्जा मिलना / फिर से जोश आ जाना

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Wasteful reckless with money
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Prodigal. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Tannery - चर्म शोधनागार - a place where animal hides are treated to produce leather. Example: The tannery in the town was known for producing high-quality leather goods. Prodigal - अपव्ययी - someone who spends money recklessly or wastefully. Example: The prodigal son spent his inheritance on lavish parties and luxury items. Valise - सूटकेस - a small suitcase or traveling bag. Example: She packed her valise and set off on her journey. Unmanly - नामर्दाना - lacking the qualities traditionally associated with men, such as courage or strength. Example: Showing emotions openly was once considered unmanly in some cultures.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. As a result, many cities struggle to balance development with environmental sustainability. B. This pattern has accelerated due to unchecked urban expansion. C. Rapid industrial growth has placed enormous pressure on natural resources. D. Forest depletion, air pollution, and water scarcity are becoming increasingly common.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. CDBA. 👉 Explanation: • C is the best opening sentence because it introduces the main cause — rapid industrial growth. • D logically follows by listing the visible consequences of this growth. • B comes next as “This pattern” refers to the environmental problems mentioned in sentence D. • A is the concluding sentence since “As a result” summarizes the final impact on cities. ✅ Final paragraph: Rapid industrial growth has placed enormous pressure on natural resources. Forest depletion, air pollution, and water scarcity are becoming increasingly common. This pattern has accelerated due to unchecked urban expansion. As a result, many cities struggle to balance development with environmental sustainability.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Then her face was bowed.
options: Then she was being bowed her face.
Her face was bowed by then.
Then her face has been bowed.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Then she bowed her face. Explanation: The given sentence "Then her face was bowed." is in the passive voice, past tense. Here, "her face" is the subject receiving the action of "was bowed." To convert it into active voice: • Identify the agent performing the action, which is "she." • Change the verb from passive ["was bowed"] to active ["bowed"]. • The object "her face" now follows the active verb. So, "Then her face was bowed." [passive] becomes "Then she bowed her face." [active]. Hence, option [4] is the correct and appropriate active voice conversion.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect/direct speech. The instructor said, “Do not submit the assignment without proofreading it.”
options: The instructor instructed not submitting the assignment without proofreading it.
The instructor told them not to submit the assignment without proofreading it.
The instructor said them to not submit the assignment without proofreading it.
The instructor warned them do not submit the assignment without proofreading it.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. The instructor told them not to submit the assignment without proofreading it. Explanation: The given sentence is in direct speech and is a negative imperative. To convert it into indirect speech: • The negative command “Do not submit” becomes “not to submit.” • A reporting verb such as “told” is used when there is a clear listener [“them”]. • The instruction is preserved in meaning without changing the tense unnecessarily. Direct speech: The instructor said, “Do not submit the assignment without proofreading it.” Indirect speech: The instructor told them not to submit the assignment without proofreading it. Hence, option [2] is the most accurate and grammatically correct transformation.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1–
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. exacerbated. 💢 Explanation - The sentence states that although growth has reduced poverty, it has also increased inequalities. Exacerbated means made worse or intensified, which accurately reflects how development can widen disparities alongside progress. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - → Option 1 – concealed means hidden, not increased. →Option 3 – neutralized means cancelled out, opposite of intended meaning. →Option 4 – stabilized means made steady, which contradicts worsening inequality.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. entrenched. 💢 Explanation - The sentence states that marginalized populations remain trapped not because opportunities do not exist, but because structural barriers remain deeply ____. This implies obstacles that are firmly established, long-standing, and resistant to change. Entrenched is commonly used in social-science contexts to describe inequalities or systems that are deeply rooted within institutions and social structures, making them extremely difficult to dismantle. Hence, it perfectly captures the persistence and rigidity of such barriers. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - → Option 2 – transient means temporary or short-lived, which contradicts the idea of long-standing structural barriers. → Option 3 – arbitrary means random or without system. Structural barriers are systematic, not random. →Option 4 – incidental means accidental or secondary, whereas these barriers are central and deeply embedded.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. escalate. 💢 Explanation - The sentence warns that policy interventions which ignore local realities may ____ social tensions rather than resolve them. The contrastive structure (rather than) indicates a negative intensification. Escalate means to increase in intensity or severity. Poorly designed policies often heighten frustration, protests, and conflict instead of easing them, making escalate the most logically precise choice. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - → Option 1 – alleviate means reduce or ease, which is opposite to the idea conveyed. → Option 2 – perpetuate means continue something at the same level, but the sentence implies worsening, not mere continuation. → Option 4 – mask means hide or conceal, which does not match the idea of increasing tensions.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. comprehensive. 💢 Explanation - Scholars emphasize reforms that address root causes instead of superficial remedies. This calls for reforms that are broad, thorough, and wide-ranging rather than narrow or partial. Comprehensive precisely conveys this idea of covering all major dimensions of a problem in an integrated manner. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - → Option 1 – cosmetic refers to surface-level changes that improve appearance but not substance. →Option 2 – piecemeal means fragmented or done in small parts, which contradicts the idea of holistic reform. →Option 4 – tentative means uncertain or hesitant, which does not match the firm approach being advocated.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. against. 💢 Explanation - The sentence states: “Only through such an approach can societies guard ____ long-term fragmentation and foster inclusive development.” The verb guard is commonly followed by the preposition against to express the idea of protection from a possible danger or negative outcome. The phrase guard against means to take preventive measures so that something harmful does not happen. In this context, societies aim to prevent or protect themselves from long-term fragmentation, making against the only grammatically and idiomatically correct choice. The construction guard against + noun (threat) is widely used in formal and academic English (e.g., guard against inflation, guard against misuse, guard against decline), further reinforcing its correctness here.
